figures.caption: TABLE I: Standard physical parameters generally used in the calculations.
FIG. 2: Schematic representation of the general gating-based architecture investigated in this work.
TABLE II: Considered neural network architectures.

FIG. 6: Top row: Isotropic distributions fθ (z,U) learned by the ANNet architecture for neon (Ne), argon (Ar), krypton (Kr), and xenon (Xe) after 10×106 iterations at standard conditions. Bottom row: Max-normalized error maps for ANNet in comparison to the reference solutions.
FIG. 5: L2-norm of the back-propagated gradients with respect to the parameters of each hidden layer ∥∇L (θ)∥2 for argon at standard conditions.

FIG. 10: Surface plots of the normalized learned isotropic distribution fθ (z,U)/ne(z) on a logarithmic scale for argon at different electric field values. The solutions are obtained using the same set of hyperparameters as in Table III.
FIG. 1: Solution domain and boundary conditions.
FIG. 13: Evolution of L1 total loss and L2 relative error for ANNet and ModNet under varying σ values.
FIG. 8: Comparison of selected macroscopic electron properties: electron density ne(z), diffusion coefficient D(z), and excitation rate coefficient kex1(z), for neon and argon at standard conditions.

FIG. 9: L2 relative errors εL2 of the isotropic and anisotropic distribution (microscopic properties) as well as the derived macroscopic electron properties across all considered gases at standard conditions. For neon, the value is the average of εL2(kex1) and εL2(kex2), which are 0.13×10−2 and 0.36×10−2, respectively. For the other gases, kex := kex1 .
TABLE III: Hyperparameters used in all PINN training cases.

FIG. 12: ANNet’s solutions upred compared against reference solutions u for (a) the Klein-Gordon equation in (x, t) space and the Helmholtz equation in (x,y) space. The rightmost panels illustrate the absolute error maps.

FIG. 7: Logarithmic slice plots of the isotropic distribution f0(z,U) normalized by the local electron densities ne(z) obtained from Eq. (8). The plots compare the reference solutions (solid lines) with the ANNet solutions (dashed lines with symbols) at z = 1, 5, and 9 cm across the four considered gases at standard conditions.
FIG. 4: Convergence behavior of the considered network architectures for argon at standard conditions and the reference solution.
FIG. 11: Ablation study on the ANNet architecture.
